surveyAnswers.q1: I noticed that most of the time they worked well, but sometimes one blocked the other chef and then they didn't get as many served, or one wasn't helping as much so less were served.
surveyAnswers.q2: I think if they were a little bit slower and all working like an assembly line where each had one job instead of all of them doing the same and blocking each other it would've flowed smoothly and then I could monitor them easier.
surveyAnswers.q3: This was a fun interesting survey! Thank you for the opportunity to try it out! Blessings on your research!

feedbackItems.feedback: it is clear that they have a good and repeatable system. the half split works well, it minimizes travel and makes efficiency the primary focus.

episodes.monitoringDifficultyReason: I thought it was very easy, because it was very easy to see that the chefs were not coordinating their actions and getting in the way of each other.

feedbackItems.reason: Just the way they are interacting so far.
feedbackItems.feedback: The chefs don't seem to be coordinated in their actions. Both chefs are taking onions to the pot, when one of them should be focusing more on grabbing a plate and waiting.
Basically both chefs get in the way of each other.
feedbackItems.reason: Just by watching the way they interact.

episodes.monitoringDifficultyReason: It was very easy to tell what the chefs were doing because the kitchen is divided in two.

feedbackItems.feedback: I think the frame shows the issue I highlighted earlier, the blue chef is stuck with a plate instead of passing more onions to green chef, and as a result green chef cannot continue after serving the plate.
feedbackItems.reason: Just observing the actions of both chefs.
Just looking at the way the blue chef remains idle with a plate instead of passing more onions for the second pot.
feedbackItems.feedback: The blue chef has control of the onions and plates and green chef prepares the pots and plating/serving, so blue chef should be focusing on passing onions at the top and plates at the bottom while the green chef picks the onions to make the soup and plate it.

episodes.monitoringDifficultyReason: It was wuite easy to tell the ways each chef actively disrupt the others by the way the kitchen is set up. There's only two passages between the two sides of the kitchen, and one of them has the pot next to it so if one of the chefs idles there waiting for the soup to be ready, they will block it; what they should all be doing is moving counterclockwise delivering onions first on both pots, then two of them should pick plates and serve. Any idling chef should be away from the passages.

feedbackItems.feedback: Yet another uncoordination example: green chef is blocking one path by waiting for the soup to be ready, blue chef does nothing, pink chef is idling while holding an onion, and orange chef placed the onion on the counter for no reason.
feedbackItems.reason: Just pointing another uncoordination example.

feedbackItems.feedback: Here we see more of that uncoordination: pink and blue chefs are blocking each other, orange chef is waiting to plate, and green chef is doing nothing.
feedbackItems.reason: Just wanted to emphasize how uncoordinated the chefs are in this episode.

feedbackItems.feedback: At this frame I can already tell how uncooordinated the chefs are. Pink chef is just idling around, while orange, green, and blue chefs grab onions and go to the same pot at the same time, blocking each other.
feedbackItems.reason: Just looking at their actions.

episodes.monitoringDifficultyReason: It was very easy to tell the poor coordination between teams and members of a team.

feedbackItems.feedback: Just another clear example of poor coordination: green chef idles instead of picking onions for the second pot, orange chef is blocking blue chef, blue chef idles instead of placing a plate on the counter for pink chef to pick up.
feedbackItems.reason: Just pointing out poor coordination between teams and members of a team.

feedbackItems.feedback: So far the teamwork seemed so-so, with one side controlling onions and plates and the other one managing cooking, plating, and serving. Green chef in particular is blocking pink chef from completing one pot; green chef should move in front of orange chef to pick onions for the second pot instead.
feedbackItems.reason: Just watching the interactions between teams and chefs.
Just the way they are interacting so far.

surveyAnswers.q3: Streamlined and user friendly survey experience.
surveyAnswers.q2: Have the plates look like plates and maybe have a pattern on them. The serving area could be more clear as it's vague as it is. Nothing stood out and all the colors were similar making it harder to remember what was what.
surveyAnswers.q1: Honestly the components weren't clear. The onions and plates looked too similar and the serving area wasn't clearly marked. I got what these were representing but if they were clearer it would have been easier to understand who was doing what. I had to remind myself what was what.
